title: Domain and Metadata Semantics
current_summary: Current focus is the accepted domain/meta bridge-DSL reset: Functional Model requirements are the source for a smaller shared meta language between domain and IO, with adapter preservation and targeted deletion of historical sediment.
next_action: Create contract-backed FTR slices that derive bridge DSL portions from Functional Model use cases, likely starting with FK-helper roundtrip; do not golden-master current implementation workarounds.
notes: This concern is a steering field, not a replacement for FTRs or concrete finding tracking. ADR-CONTRACT-FIRST-DOMAIN-META-REWRITE records the counter-conservative direction: low adoption makes breaking domain/meta changes acceptable when they reduce sediment and stabilize the bridge DSL.

title: Adapter and Spreadsheet Boundary
current_summary: Current focus is adapter layer shape, spreadsheet boundary, layout/render/write/read semantics, and workbook option vocabulary.
next_action: Watch residual seam-hardening and vocabulary findings while domain/meta remains the louder hotspot.
notes: Not closed; current pressure is lower than domain/meta semantics.

title: User Surface and Adoption
current_summary: Current focus is tutorials, user guide, demo path, release/public surface, onboarding, and adoption confidence.
next_action: Review after the next public-surface or release-preparation slice rather than turning every low-priority doc signal into a task.
notes: REVIEW-003 made this visible as a real concern thread, but it is not the current highest-pressure implementation slice.

title: Projection Ergonomics
current_summary: Current focus is dynamic projections, workbook views, crosstables, editing-surface ergonomics, and model-to-projection workflows.
next_action: Keep projection ergonomics tied to concrete dynamic-view and workbook-view work; avoid redesigning projection mechanisms without a slice.
notes: Active as a strategic pressure field, but many concrete changes should wait for domain/meta clarification.

title: Backend Portability
current_summary: Current focus is XLSX/ODS parity, CSV/XML/JSON limitations, LibreOffice behavior, and backend capability clarity.
next_action: Keep as a watch concern until a consumer or release slice creates concrete backend pressure.
notes: Current evidence supports awareness rather than a dedicated portability wave.

title: Maintainability Hygiene
current_summary: Current focus is module size, package surfaces, module headers, localized duplication, and readable code boundaries.
next_action: Review after focused refactor slices; do not run a mechanical cleanup wave without concrete pressure.
notes: Many low/medium findings are heatmap signals for this concern rather than individual tasks.

title: Project Memory Governance
current_summary: Current focus is project_memory structure, review/finding/concern modeling, and roundtrip-friendly governance data.
next_action: Keep the canonical model flat and spreadsheet-roundtrip-friendly while adding only evidence-backed steering structure.
notes: This thread covers the dogfood governance model itself.
